title: Graphs & Pathfinding
description: Understand graphs, adjacency matrices, DFS, BFS, Dijkstra, and A* pathfinding for AI navigation.
learningObjectives: Represent graphs using adjacency matrices and lists
Traverse graphs using Depth-First Search (DFS) and Breadth-First Search (BFS)
Implement Dijkstra's and A* heuristics for AI pathfinding on game grids

sections.title: Graphs and AI Navigation
sections.content: Graphs model connections between nodes (vertices) linked by edges. In game engines, pathfinding systems (NavMesh) represent walkable geometry as a graph. AI navigation algorithms find paths through this grid: BFS finds the shortest path on unweighted graphs, Dijkstra handles weighted edges (e.g. mud slows movement), and A* adds a distance heuristic (estimated cost to target) to find paths significantly faster.

sections.title: A* Heuristic Logic
